addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  The heat's keystone membrane — small surface, subtle exit-status semantics;
  every later negative assertion rides through it.
  Tier: opus-driven.
  
  ## Goal
  Establish the precision exit-code band and make `buc_die` transparent to it.
  
  Define band tinder in `Tools/buk/bubc_constants.sh`:
  band base/width plus per-gate rejection codes —
  per rejection gate, never per validation rule.
  Pick the band clear of shell-reserved codes (2, 126, 127, 128+n).
  
  Teach `buc_die` (`Tools/buk/buc_command.sh`) to capture `$?` on entry
  and re-exit with it when the value lies in the band;
  add an origin helper for deliberate rejection sites.
  Existing `cmd || buc_die` call sites stay untouched —
  the membrane makes them propagate band codes for free.
  
  Prove end-to-end survival:
  a BUK self-test case where a band code raised under a `cmd || buc_die` chain
  reaches the caller's captured exit status through the tabtarget/launcher exec path.
  
  ## Cinched
  Exit-code band over stderr sentinel;
  per-gate codes, not per-rule.
  
  ## Done when
  Band tinder and membrane land;
  `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green including the new survival case.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣Be
2	
3	## Gate
4	
5	Cleared (2026-06-12): the bash-filename case-consolidation landed and its heat retired;
6	this heat's docket file references have been refreshed to the lowercase successors.
7	This heat runs comfortably in a parallel repo alongside ₣Bb.
8	
9	## Shape
10	
11	Two strands, one gestalt: doctrine-pure negative testing.
12	
13	Strand one — named refusals.
14	Theurge negative cases assert bare nonzero exit,
15	so a case expecting deliberate rejection also passes on any harness breakage
16	(unbound variable, missing file, refactor typo) —
17	the fast-tier fixtures carry this wrong-reason hole across their negative cases.
18	The fix: a small precision exit-code band, allocated per rejection gate
19	(roughly half a dozen codes; the census at the 2026-06-11 groom counted 6–8 gates),
20	homed as tinder in bubc,
21	projected into the theurge Rust consts by the existing zipper codegen,
22	and asserted specifically by the negative helpers.
23	
24	Strand two — the tweak channel re-grounded (added at the 2026-06-11 groom).
25	The BUS0 Tweak Mechanism doctrine (landed at heat start) states what a tweak is for:
26	force one hard-to-produce condition for a test to observe handled correctly;
27	one tweak at a time per test/fixture/suite, by design;
28	a suite may reserve the slot for a standing guard.
29	This heat brings the live census into conformance:
30	the two non-conforming tweaks retire
31	(the immure convenience short-circuit becomes a real read-only dry-run colophon;
32	the graft parameter injection becomes the hallmark-installer election chain,
33	riding the existing hallmark fact + previous-dir chaining machinery),
34	the fast tier reserves the slot for the credless guard
35	(closing the recorded near-miss class: a passing fast suite that spends money and mutates the depot,
36	gated at the token-mint chokepoint with a band code),
37	and the regime-validation negatives convert from fabricated files driven through
38	test-only `*_probate` side doors to in-universe poisoning —
39	real validate verbs against real regimes, one BUK regime-load seam with set/unset semantics —
40	housed in a new fixture that runs in every suite above fast
41	(fast holds the guard; poison cases need the slot; the two never share a run).
42	
43	Work order: census cleanup first (band-independent),
44	then band membrane + numeric codegen,
45	then the guard (its rejection code needs the band),
46	then the conversions, prose last.
47	
48	## Cinched
49	
50	- Exit-code band over stderr sentinel:
51	  a sentinel is string interpretation minimized, not avoided,
52	  and is swallowed by the same wrappers that launder codes.
53	- Per-gate codes, never per-rule —
54	  the hole being closed is wrong-layer failure, not wrong-rule failure.
55	- Allocation rule: gates may share a code only if they never co-occur
56	  in one test case's spawn path — share across alternatives, never along a pipeline.
57	- `buc_die` propagates in-band `$?` values instead of remapping to 1,
58	  so existing `|| buc_die` call sites need no audit or change.
59	- No code minted outside the bubc tinder block.
60	- One tweak at a time is doctrine, not limitation;
61	  a dedicated constraint variable mints only when a genuine dual-tweak need survives scrutiny —
62	  never by widening the single slot.
63	- Fast carries the credless guard in the tweak slot;
64	  fast cases carry no tweaks of their own.
65	  A case that needs a seam has self-identified as not belonging in fast.
66	- In-universe over fabrication: negatives run real verbs against real regimes wherever the
67	  injection point is a value; the probate side doors delete with no parallel survival.
68	- The guard gates token mint (actual credential use), never zipper/dispatch — zippers untouched.
69	- The hallmark installer is graft-slot election only;
70	  any general election verb belongs to the made-side retrofit heat.
71	
72	## Out of scope
73	
74	- tadmor-security's containment cases:
75	  exit codes there cross docker-exec boundaries with their own laundering rules —
76	  a different problem, a different heat if it ever itches.
77	- enrollment-validation needs no in-universe conversion:
78	  it already stages values inline against the validator with no file fabrication and no tweak;
79	  it takes only the band-code assertion migration.
80	- recipe-validation and dockerfile-hygiene fabrication is honest:
81	  the artifact under test IS a file; they take only the band-code migration.
82	
83	## Done when
84	
85	Every fast-tier negative case asserts a specific band code,
86	the survival proof lives in the BUK self-test,
87	the tweak census conforms to the BUS0 doctrine (stamp, poison seam, guard — nothing else),
88	no regime `*_probate` side door survives,
89	fast is credless by construction,
90	and the BCG admission entry documents band semantics and the allocation rule.
91	
92	# jjezs_pace ₢BeAAA
93	
94	## Character
95	The heat's keystone membrane — small surface, subtle exit-status semantics;
96	every later negative assertion rides through it.
97	Tier: opus-driven.
98	
99	## Goal
100	Establish the precision exit-code band and make `buc_die` transparent to it.
101	
102	Define band tinder in `Tools/buk/bubc_constants.sh`:
103	band base/width plus per-gate rejection codes —
104	per rejection gate, never per validation rule.
105	Pick the band clear of shell-reserved codes (2, 126, 127, 128+n).
106	
107	Teach `buc_die` (`Tools/buk/buc_command.sh`) to capture `$?` on entry
108	and re-exit with it when the value lies in the band;
109	add an origin helper for deliberate rejection sites.
110	Existing `cmd || buc_die` call sites stay untouched —
111	the membrane makes them propagate band codes for free.
112	
113	Prove end-to-end survival:
114	a BUK self-test case where a band code raised under a `cmd || buc_die` chain
115	reaches the caller's captured exit status through the tabtarget/launcher exec path.
116	
117	## Cinched
118	Exit-code band over stderr sentinel;
119	per-gate codes, not per-rule.
120	
121	## Done when
122	Band tinder and membrane land;
123	`tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green including the new survival case.

# jjezs_paddock ₣Be

## Gate

Cleared (2026-06-12): the bash-filename case-consolidation landed and its heat retired;
this heat's docket file references have been refreshed to the lowercase successors.
This heat runs comfortably in a parallel repo alongside ₣Bb.

## Shape

Two strands, one gestalt: doctrine-pure negative testing.

Strand one — named refusals.
Theurge negative cases assert bare nonzero exit,
so a case expecting deliberate rejection also passes on any harness breakage
(unbound variable, missing file, refactor typo) —
the fast-tier fixtures carry this wrong-reason hole across their negative cases.
The fix: a small precision exit-code band, allocated per rejection gate
(roughly half a dozen codes; the census at the 2026-06-11 groom counted 6–8 gates),
homed as tinder in bubc,
projected into the theurge Rust consts by the existing zipper codegen,
and asserted specifically by the negative helpers.

Strand two — the tweak channel re-grounded (added at the 2026-06-11 groom).
The BUS0 Tweak Mechanism doctrine (landed at heat start) states what a tweak is for:
force one hard-to-produce condition for a test to observe handled correctly;
one tweak at a time per test/fixture/suite, by design;
a suite may reserve the slot for a standing guard.
This heat brings the live census into conformance:
the two non-conforming tweaks retire
(the immure convenience short-circuit becomes a real read-only dry-run colophon;
the graft parameter injection becomes the hallmark-installer election chain,
riding the existing hallmark fact + previous-dir chaining machinery),
the fast tier reserves the slot for the credless guard
(closing the recorded near-miss class: a passing fast suite that spends money and mutates the depot,
gated at the token-mint chokepoint with a band code),
and the regime-validation negatives convert from fabricated files driven through
test-only `*_probate` side doors to in-universe poisoning —
real validate verbs against real regimes, one BUK regime-load seam with set/unset semantics —
housed in a new fixture that runs in every suite above fast
(fast holds the guard; poison cases need the slot; the two never share a run).

Work order: census cleanup first (band-independent),
then band membrane + numeric codegen,
then the guard (its rejection code needs the band),
then the conversions, prose last.

## Cinched

- Exit-code band over stderr sentinel:
  a sentinel is string interpretation minimized, not avoided,
  and is swallowed by the same wrappers that launder codes.
- Per-gate codes, never per-rule —
  the hole being closed is wrong-layer failure, not wrong-rule failure.
- Allocation rule: gates may share a code only if they never co-occur
  in one test case's spawn path — share across alternatives, never along a pipeline.
- `buc_die` propagates in-band `$?` values instead of remapping to 1,
  so existing `|| buc_die` call sites need no audit or change.
- No code minted outside the bubc tinder block.
- One tweak at a time is doctrine, not limitation;
  a dedicated constraint variable mints only when a genuine dual-tweak need survives scrutiny —
  never by widening the single slot.
- Fast carries the credless guard in the tweak slot;
  fast cases carry no tweaks of their own.
  A case that needs a seam has self-identified as not belonging in fast.
- In-universe over fabrication: negatives run real verbs against real regimes wherever the
  injection point is a value; the probate side doors delete with no parallel survival.
- The guard gates token mint (actual credential use), never zipper/dispatch — zippers untouched.
- The hallmark installer is graft-slot election only;
  any general election verb belongs to the made-side retrofit heat.

## Out of scope

- tadmor-security's containment cases:
  exit codes there cross docker-exec boundaries with their own laundering rules —
  a different problem, a different heat if it ever itches.
- enrollment-validation needs no in-universe conversion:
  it already stages values inline against the validator with no file fabrication and no tweak;
  it takes only the band-code assertion migration.
- recipe-validation and dockerfile-hygiene fabrication is honest:
  the artifact under test IS a file; they take only the band-code migration.

## Done when

Every fast-tier negative case asserts a specific band code,
the survival proof lives in the BUK self-test,
the tweak census conforms to the BUS0 doctrine (stamp, poison seam, guard — nothing else),
no regime `*_probate` side door survives,
fast is credless by construction,
and the BCG admission entry documents band semantics and the allocation rule.

# jjezs_pace ₢BeAAA

## Character
The heat's keystone membrane — small surface, subtle exit-status semantics;
every later negative assertion rides through it.
Tier: opus-driven.

## Goal
Establish the precision exit-code band and make `buc_die` transparent to it.

Define band tinder in `Tools/buk/bubc_constants.sh`:
band base/width plus per-gate rejection codes —
per rejection gate, never per validation rule.
Pick the band clear of shell-reserved codes (2, 126, 127, 128+n).

Teach `buc_die` (`Tools/buk/buc_command.sh`) to capture `$?` on entry
and re-exit with it when the value lies in the band;
add an origin helper for deliberate rejection sites.
Existing `cmd || buc_die` call sites stay untouched —
the membrane makes them propagate band codes for free.

Prove end-to-end survival:
a BUK self-test case where a band code raised under a `cmd || buc_die` chain
reaches the caller's captured exit status through the tabtarget/launcher exec path.

## Cinched
Exit-code band over stderr sentinel;
per-gate codes, not per-rule.

## Done when
Band tinder and membrane land;
`tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green including the new survival case.

content.text:
**₢BeAAA (band-tinder-and-die-membrane): establish the precision exit-code band — tinder constants in `bubc_constants.sh`, a `buc_die` membrane that re-exits in-band `$?` instead of remapping to 1, and a BUK self-test case proving a band code survives the full tabtarget/launcher exec path.**

Heat context: ₣Be (rbk-09-better-negatives) is doctrine-pure negative testing — 2/10 paces done (the two tweak retirements landed); this is the keystone membrane every later negative assertion rides through. The silks fit the docket.

Approach: opus-tier per the docket, sequential single-agent — read `bubc_constants.sh`, `buc_command.sh`, and the BUK self-test fixture; pick the band; wire the membrane; add the survival case; run `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh`.

Proceed?
Starting with reading the key files.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#

#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUBC — BUK Bootstrap Constants
# Source-time literal constants for BUK kit layout.
# No kindle dependency — available immediately upon sourcing.

# Guard against multiple inclusion
test -z "${ZBUBC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUBC_SOURCED=1

# Source-time literal constants.
#
# BUBC_moorings_dir is the basename of the project's config dir. The
# project-intimate trampoline (z-launcher) is the sole authority for the name;
# it exports BURD_CONFIG_DIR (absolute), and we derive the basename here so
# tooling (qualify, tabtarget creation) refers to it without re-literaling.
BUBC_moorings_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR##*/}"
BUBC_launchers_subdir="rbml_launchers"
BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir="rbmu_users"

# Platform identifiers (bunne_* — BURN node-regime enum sprue family).
# These values are the canonical OS-family identifiers used in BURN_PLATFORM
# enrollment and in per-tabtarget platform invariant assertions.
# BUBC_platforms_<family> tinder constants provide single source of truth so
# code refers to the identifier by family name rather than hardcoding the
# literal token at every comparison site.
BUBC_platforms_linux="bunne_linux"
BUBC_platforms_mac="bunne_mac"
BUBC_platforms_windows="bunne_windows"

# Windows OpenSSH layout — forward slashes throughout so identical strings
# work in PowerShell, terminal display, and icacls invocations.
BUBC_windows_sshd_config='C:/ProgramData/ssh/sshd_config'
BUBC_windows_admin_auth_keys='C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys'
BUBC_windows_ssh_port="22"
BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name="sshd"
BUBC_windows_fw_display_name="OpenSSH Server"

# Windows registry preconditions for unattended power-on posture.
# Operator-handbook step (BUSJHW Windows: Host Availability) sets these;
# bujb_invigilate_windows reads them. Single source of truth so the path
# the handbook tells the operator to set is the path invigilate queries.
# PowerShell-canonical form (HKLM:\ prefix, mixed case — registry is
# case-insensitive at the OS level so display case is purely cosmetic).
BUBC_windows_passwordless_path='HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device'
BUBC_windows_passwordless_value='DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion'
BUBC_windows_aoac_path='HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power'
BUBC_windows_aoac_value='PlatformAoAcOverride'

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	#
10	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	# limitations under the License.
15	#
16	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
17	#
18	# Bash Console Utility Library
19	
20	set -euo pipefail
21	
22	# Multiple inclusion guard
23	test -z "${ZBUC_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
24	ZBUC_INCLUDED=1
25	
26	# Color is no longer buc's own concern.  Every display path renders through
27	# the buym core (buyf_format_yawp over the kindle-time BUYC_* palette), which
28	# owns the single terminal-capability decision and honors NO_COLOR/TERM=dumb.
29	# Semantic ambients flow in by BUYC_* name (resolved after kindle); colored
30	# prefixes ride buym's WARN/FAIL span markers.
31	
32	# Global context variable for info and error messages
33	ZBUC_CONTEXT=""
34	
35	# Help mode flag
36	ZBUC_DOC_MODE=false
37	
38	
39	######################################################################
40	# Internal logging helpers
41	
42	# Usage: zbuc_make_tag <depth> "<label>"
43	#   Computes ZBUC_TAG for the given stack depth/label (no I/O).
44	# Usage: zbuc_tag_args <depth> "<label>" [arg...]
45	#   Computes ZBUC_TAG and logs args directly to the transcript.
46	#
47	# Bash stack quirk:
48	#   BASH_SOURCE[i] / FUNCNAME[i] index the current frame,
49	#   but BASH_LINENO[i] reports the line where FUNCNAME[i+1] was called.
50	#   For depth=N callers:
51	#       file = BASH_SOURCE[N]
52	#       line = BASH_LINENO[N-1]
53	# Usage: zbuc_make_tag <depth> "<label>"
54	#   Computes ZBUC_TAG for the given stack depth/label (no I/O).
55	#   Note: With depth=0 or too-deep stacks, file/line may be empty (by design).
56	zbuc_make_tag() {
57	  local z_d="${1:-1}"
58	  case "${z_d}" in ''|*[!0-9]*) z_d=1 ;; esac
59	  local z_label="${2:-}"
60	  local z_file="${BASH_SOURCE[z_d]##*/}"
61	  local z_line="${BASH_LINENO[z_d-1]}"
62	  ZBUC_TAG="${z_label}${z_file}:${z_line}: "
63	}
64	
65	zbuc_tag_args() {
66	  local z_d="${1:-1}"
67	  shift
68	  local z_label="${1:-}"
69	  shift
70	  zbuc_make_tag "${z_d}" "${z_label}"
71	  local z_arg
72	  for z_arg in "$@"; do
73	    buyf_strip_yawp "${z_arg}"
74	    printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}"
75	  done | zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "
76	}
77	
78	######################################################################
79	# Public logging wrappers
80	
81	buc_log_args() { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_log_args " "$@"; }
82	buc_log_pipe() { zbuc_make_tag 3 "buc_log_pipe "; zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "; }
83	
84	buc_step()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_step     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE "$*"; }
85	buc_code()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_code     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 BUYC_CYAN         "$*"; }
86	buc_info()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_info     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 ""                "$@"; }
87	buc_debug()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_debug    " "$@"; zbuc_print 2 ""                "$@"; }
88	buc_trace()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_trace    " "$@"; zbuc_print 3 ""                "$@"; }
89	buc_warn() {
90	  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_warn     " "$@"
91	  buyy_warn_yawp "WARNING:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
92	  zbuc_print 0 "" "${z_pfx} $*"
93	}
94	buc_success() {
95	  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_success  " "$@"
96	  zbuc_tint BUYC_GREEN "$*"
97	  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2 || buc_die
98	}
99	buc_die() {
100	  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_die      " "ERROR: [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
101	  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
102	  zbuc_print -1 "" "${z_pfx} [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
103	  exit 1
104	}
105	
106	# Display unprefixed cyan text for typeable guidance (commands, config values).
107	# Sigil-less by design — routes color through the core but skips the context tag.
108	buc_bare() {
109	  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_bare     " "$@"
110	  zbuc_tint BUYC_CYAN "$*"
111	  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2
112	}
113	
114	# Display tabtarget hint: resolves colophon to tabtarget filename
115	# Args: colophon [extra_args...]
116	buc_tabtarget() {
117	  local z_colophon="$1"
118	  shift
119	  local z_extra="${*:+ $*}"
120	  zbuym_tt_path "${z_colophon}"
121	  test -n "${z_buym_tt_path}" || buc_die "buc_tabtarget: no tabtarget found for colophon '${z_colophon}'"
122	  buc_bare "        ${z_buym_tt_path}${z_extra}"
123	}
124	
125	buc_context() {
126	  ZBUC_CONTEXT="$1"
127	}
128	
129	# Enable trace to stderr safely if supported
130	zbuc_enable_trace() {
131	  # Only supported in Bash >= 4.1
132	  if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
133	    export BASH_XTRACEFD=2
134	  fi
135	  set -x
136	}
137	
138	# Disable trace
139	zbuc_disable_trace() {
140	  set +x
141	}
142	
143	zbuc_doc_mode_predicate() {
144	  test "${ZBUC_DOC_MODE}" = "true"
145	}
146	
147	buc_doc_env() {
148	  set -e
149	
150	  local env_var_name="${1}"
151	  local env_var_info="${2}"
152	
153	  # Trim trailing spaces from variable name
154	  env_var_name="${env_var_name%% *}"
155	
156	  # In doc mode, show documentation only (no validation — env vars may not be set)
157	  if zbuc_doc_mode_predicate; then
158	    zbuc_tint BUYC_MAGENTA "${1}"
159	    echo "  ${z_buym_format}:  ${env_var_info}"
160	    return 0
161	  fi
162	
163	  # In execute mode, validate variable is set
164	  test -n "${!env_var_name:-}" || buc_warn "${env_var_name} is not set"
165	}
166	
167	# Idiomatic last step of environment documentation in furnish.
168	# In doc mode, signals furnish to return early (sourcing/kindle not needed for help).
169	# Usage:
170	#    buc_doc_env_done || return 0
171	buc_doc_env_done() {
172	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
173	  return 1
174	}
175	
176	ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="UNFILLED"
177	
178	buc_doc_brief() {
179	  set -e
180	  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"
181	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
182	  echo
183	  zbuc_tint BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"
184	  echo "  ${z_buym_format}"
185	  echo "    brief: $1"
186	}
187	
188	buc_doc_lines() {
189	  set -e
190	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
191	  echo "           $1"
192	}
193	
194	buc_doc_param() {
195	  set -e
196	  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING} <<$1>>"
197	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
198	  echo "    required: $1 - $2"
199	}
200	
201	buc_doc_oparm() {
202	  set -e
203	  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING} [<<$1>>]"
204	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
205	  echo "    optional: $1 - $2"
206	}
207	
208	zbuc_usage() {
209	  zbuc_tint BUYC_CYAN "${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING}"
210	  printf '    usage: %s\n' "${z_buym_format}"
211	}
212	
213	# Idiomatic last step of documentation in the bash api.
214	# Usage:
215	#    buc_doc_shown || return 0
216	buc_doc_shown() {
217	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
218	  zbuc_usage
219	  return 1
220	}
221	
222	buc_set_doc_mode() {
223	  ZBUC_DOC_MODE=true
224	}
225	
226	buc_usage_die() {
227	  set -e
228	  local usage; usage=$(zbuc_usage)
229	  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
230	  zbuc_tint "" "${z_pfx} ${usage}"
231	  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}"
232	  exit 1
233	}
234	
235	# Tint <text> through the buym core with the ambient named by <BUYC_name>
236	# (empty name => default/no ambient).  Sentinel-guarded and name-indirect so
237	# every BUYC_* dereference happens after kindle — a cold caller (doc-mode,
238	# pre-kindle buc_die) is set -u safe.  Resolved string lands in z_buym_format.
239	zbuc_tint() {
240	  zbuym_sentinel
241	  local z_name="${1:-}"
242	  local z_ambient=""
243	  test -z "${z_name}" || z_ambient="${!z_name}"
244	  buyf_format_yawp "${z_ambient}" "${2:-}"
245	}
246	
247	# Multi-line print function with verbosity control.
248	# Usage: zbuc_print <min_verbosity> <BUYC_ambient_name> [message...]
249	# Sends output to stderr to avoid interfering with stdout returns.  Each
250	# message renders through the shared buym resolver (via zbuc_tint), so inline
251	# yawp spans resolve, the named ambient and gray operation sigil are
252	# terminal-aware, and a cold display does not trip set -u.
253	zbuc_print() {
254	  local min_verbosity="$1"
255	  local ambient_name="$2"
256	  shift 2
257	
258	  # Always print if min_verbosity is -1, otherwise check BURE_VERBOSE
259	  if test "${min_verbosity}" -eq -1 || test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge "${min_verbosity}"; then
260	    zbuym_sentinel
261	    while test $# -gt 0; do
262	      zbuc_tint "${ambient_name}" "$1"
263	      if test -n "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"; then
264	        printf '%s%s%s %s\n' "${BUYC_GRAY}" "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}" "${BUYC_RESET}" "${z_buym_format}" >&2
265	      else
266	        printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2
267	      fi
268	      shift
269	    done
270	  fi
271	}
272	
273	# Core logging implementation - always reads from stdin
274	zbuc_log() {
275	  test -n "${BURD_TRANSCRIPT:-}" || return 0
276	
277	  local z_prefix="$1"
278	  local z_rest_prefix="$2"
279	  local z_outfile="${BURD_TRANSCRIPT}"
280	
281	  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
282	    printf '%s%s\n' "${z_prefix}" "${z_line}" >> "${z_outfile}"
283	    z_prefix="${z_rest_prefix}"
284	  done
285	}
286	
287	
288	# Die if condition is true (non-zero)
289	# Usage: buc_die_if <condition> <message1> [<message2> ...]
290	buc_die_if() {
291	  local condition="$1"
292	  shift
293	
294	  test "${condition}" -ne 0 || return 0
295	
296	  set -e
297	  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
298	  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
299	  zbuc_print -1 "" "${z_pfx} [$context] $1"
300	  shift
301	  zbuc_print -1 "" "$@"
302	  exit 1
303	}
304	
305	# Die unless condition is true (zero)
306	# Usage: buc_die_unless <condition> <message1> [<message2> ...]
307	buc_die_unless() {
308	  local condition="$1"
309	  shift
310	
311	  test "${condition}" -eq 0 || return 0
312	
313	  set -e
314	  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
315	  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
316	  zbuc_print -1 "" "${z_pfx} [$context] $1"
317	  shift
318	  zbuc_print -1 "" "$@"
319	  exit 1
320	}
321	
322	zbuc_show_help() {
323	  local prefix="$1"
324	  local title="$2"
325	  local env_func="$3"
326	
327	  echo "$title"
328	  echo
329	
330	  if test -n "${env_func}"; then
331	    echo "Environment Variables:"
332	    "$env_func"
333	    echo
334	  fi
335	
336	  echo "Commands:"
337	
338	  local z_decl z_flag z_cmd
339	  while read -r z_decl z_flag z_cmd; do
340	    [[ "${z_cmd}" =~ ^${prefix}[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ ]] || continue
341	    buc_context "${z_cmd}"
342	    "${z_cmd}"
343	  done < <(declare -F)
344	}
345	
346	buc_countdown() {
347	  local z_seconds="$1"
348	  local z_prompt="$2"
349	
350	  if test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" = "skip"; then
351	    buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s (skipped — BURE_COUNTDOWN=skip)"
352	    return 0
353	  fi
354	
355	  test -z "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"
356	
357	  buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s to cancel (Ctrl-C)"
358	  sleep 1
359	  zbuc_tint BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW "${z_prompt}"
360	  printf '%s ' "${z_buym_format}" >/dev/tty
361	  local z_i
362	  for (( z_i=z_seconds; z_i>=1; z_i-- )); do
363	    printf '%d... ' "$z_i" >/dev/tty
364	    sleep 1
365	  done
366	  printf '\n' >/dev/tty
367	}
368	
369	buc_require() {
370	  local z_prompt="$1"
371	  local z_required_value="$2"
372	
373	  if test "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" = "skip"; then
374	    buc_step "Confirm: (skipped — BURE_CONFIRM=skip)"
375	    return 0
376	  fi
377	
378	  test -z "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" || buc_die "BURE_CONFIRM must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_CONFIRM}'"
379	
380	  sleep 1
381	  zbuc_tint BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW "${z_prompt}"
382	  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2
383	  printf 'Type %s to confirm: ' "${z_required_value}" >&2
384	  local z_input
385	  read -r z_input </dev/tty
386	  test "${z_input}" = "${z_required_value}" || buc_die "Confirmation failed — expected '${z_required_value}', got '${z_input}'"
387	}
388	
389	buc_execute() {
390	  set -e
391	  local prefix="$1"
392	  local title="$2"
393	  local env_func="$3"
394	  local command="${4:-}"
395	  shift 3; test -z "${command}" || shift
396	
397	  export BUC_VERBOSE="${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}"
398	
399	  # Enable bash trace to stderr if BUC_VERBOSE is 3 or higher and bash >= 4.1
400	  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE}" -ge 3; then
401	    if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
402	      export PS4='+ ${BASH_SOURCE##*/}:${LINENO}: '
403	      export BASH_XTRACEFD=2
404	      set -x
405	    fi
406	  fi
407	
408	  # Validate prefix pattern, furnish deps, then dispatch command
409	  if test -n "${command}" && [[ "${command}" =~ ^${prefix}[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ ]]; then
410	    buc_context "${command}"
411	    test -z "${env_func}" || "${env_func}" "${command}"
412	    declare -F "${command}" >/dev/null || buc_die "Function not found: ${command}"
413	    "${command}" "$@"
414	  else
415	    test -z "${command}" || buc_warn "Unknown command: ${command}"
416	    buc_set_doc_mode
417	    zbuc_show_help "${prefix}" "${title}" "${env_func}"
418	    echo
419	    exit 1
420	  fi
421	}
422	
423	# --- Hyperlink helpers (OSC-8), falls back to plain text when disabled ---
424	# Disable with: export BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS=1
425	zbuc_hyperlink() {
426	  local z_text="${1:-}"
427	  local z_url="${2:-}"
428	
429	  # ANSI codes for blue underlined text
430	  local z_blue_underline=$'\033[34m\033[4m'
431	  local z_reset=$'\033[0m'
432	
433	  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
434	    # Fallback: blue underlined text with URL in angle brackets
435	    printf '%s%s%s <%s>' "${z_blue_underline}" "${z_text}" "${z_reset}" "${z_url}"
436	    return 0
437	  fi
438	
439	  # OSC-8 with blue underline formatting
440	  printf '%s\033]8;;%s\033\\%s\033]8;;\033\\%s' \
441	    "${z_blue_underline}" "${z_url}" "${z_text}" "${z_reset}"
442	}
443	
444	# buc_link "Prefix text" "Link text" "URL"  (prints to stderr like other user-visible messages)
445	buc_link() {
446	  local z_prefix="${1:-}"
447	  local z_text="${2:-}"
448	  local z_url="${3:-}"
449	
450	  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_link    " "${z_prefix} ${z_text} -> ${z_url}"
451	
452	  # Always show at verbosity >= 0 (same visibility as buc_step)
453	  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 0; then
454	    # Print prefix text if provided
455	    test -n "${z_prefix}" && printf '%s ' "${z_prefix}" >&2
456	
457	    # Print formatted hyperlink
458	    zbuc_hyperlink "${z_text}" "${z_url}" >&2
459	    echo >&2
460	  fi
461	}
462	
463	# --- Native path translation (Cygwin -> Windows-native) ---
464	#
465	# Normalize a path argument for a Windows-native tool (docker, cargo, ...)
466	# invoked from Cygwin bash. A Windows-native binary reads a Cygwin /cygdrive/X/...
467	# path as a literal Windows path and reports "does not exist"; hand it the
468	# drive-letter form (X:/... — forward slashes, which Windows accepts). Pure
469	# /cygdrive parameter expansion, no cygpath subshell, mirroring RBTDRX's Rust
470	# fast path. Gated on BURD_OSTYPE (the dispatch-synthesized platform fact): off
471	# Cygwin every path is emitted unchanged. A relative or already-native path
472	# passes through; a bare-absolute POSIX path (leading / but not /cygdrive) is an
473	# unsurveyed shape and returns 1 so the caller dies. Reads only its argument and
474	# BURD_OSTYPE — no kindle state — so it stays unit-testable in isolation.
475	#
476	# This is the single home for the transform: it folds the formerly-duplicated
477	# per-module copies (zrbfc/zrbndb/zrbob_native_path_capture) into one (heat ₣BV).
478	# Retire when the Windows-native tools build/run as true Cygwin binaries.
479	buc_native_path_capture() {
480	  local -r z_path="${1:?buc_native_path_capture: path required}"
481	
482	  if test "${BURD_OSTYPE:-}" != "cygwin"; then
483	    printf '%s\n' "${z_path}"
484	    return 0
485	  fi
486	
487	  case "${z_path}" in
488	    /cygdrive/?/*)
489	      local -r z_drive_rest="${z_path#/cygdrive/}"
490	      local -r z_drive="${z_drive_rest%%/*}"
491	      local -r z_drive_tail="${z_drive_rest#"${z_drive}/"}"
492	      printf '%s\n' "${z_drive}:/${z_drive_tail}"
493	      ;;
494	    /*)
495	      return 1
496	      ;;
497	    *)
498	      printf '%s\n' "${z_path}"
499	      ;;
500	  esac
501	}
502	
503	# eof
504

#!/bin/bash
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Console Utility Library

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUC_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
ZBUC_INCLUDED=1

# Color is no longer buc's own concern.  Every display path renders through
# the buym core (buyf_format_yawp over the kindle-time BUYC_* palette), which
# owns the single terminal-capability decision and honors NO_COLOR/TERM=dumb.
# Semantic ambients flow in by BUYC_* name (resolved after kindle); colored
# prefixes ride buym's WARN/FAIL span markers.

# Global context variable for info and error messages
ZBUC_CONTEXT=""

# Help mode flag
ZBUC_DOC_MODE=false


######################################################################
# Internal logging helpers

# Usage: zbuc_make_tag <depth> "<label>"
#   Computes ZBUC_TAG for the given stack depth/label (no I/O).
# Usage: zbuc_tag_args <depth> "<label>" [arg...]
#   Computes ZBUC_TAG and logs args directly to the transcript.
#
# Bash stack quirk:
#   BASH_SOURCE[i] / FUNCNAME[i] index the current frame,
#   but BASH_LINENO[i] reports the line where FUNCNAME[i+1] was called.
#   For depth=N callers:
#       file = BASH_SOURCE[N]
#       line = BASH_LINENO[N-1]
# Usage: zbuc_make_tag <depth> "<label>"
#   Computes ZBUC_TAG for the given stack depth/label (no I/O).
#   Note: With depth=0 or too-deep stacks, file/line may be empty (by design).
zbuc_make_tag() {
  local z_d="${1:-1}"
  case "${z_d}" in ''|*[!0-9]*) z_d=1 ;; esac
  local z_label="${2:-}"
  local z_file="${BASH_SOURCE[z_d]##*/}"
  local z_line="${BASH_LINENO[z_d-1]}"
  ZBUC_TAG="${z_label}${z_file}:${z_line}: "
}

zbuc_tag_args() {
  local z_d="${1:-1}"
  shift
  local z_label="${1:-}"
  shift
  zbuc_make_tag "${z_d}" "${z_label}"
  local z_arg
  for z_arg in "$@"; do
    buyf_strip_yawp "${z_arg}"
    printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}"
  done | zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "
}

######################################################################
# Public logging wrappers

buc_log_args() { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_log_args " "$@"; }
buc_log_pipe() { zbuc_make_tag 3 "buc_log_pipe "; zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "; }

buc_step()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_step     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE "$*"; }
buc_code()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_code     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 BUYC_CYAN         "$*"; }
buc_info()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_info     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 ""                "$@"; }
buc_debug()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_debug    " "$@"; zbuc_print 2 ""                "$@"; }
buc_trace()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_trace    " "$@"; zbuc_print 3 ""                "$@"; }
buc_warn() {
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_warn     " "$@"
  buyy_warn_yawp "WARNING:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
  zbuc_print 0 "" "${z_pfx} $*"
}
buc_success() {
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_success  " "$@"
  zbuc_tint BUYC_GREEN "$*"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2 || buc_die
}
buc_die() {
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_die      " "ERROR: [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
  zbuc_print -1 "" "${z_pfx} [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
  exit 1
}

# Display unprefixed cyan text for typeable guidance (commands, config values).
# Sigil-less by design — routes color through the core but skips the context tag.
buc_bare() {
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_bare     " "$@"
  zbuc_tint BUYC_CYAN "$*"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

# Display tabtarget hint: resolves colophon to tabtarget filename
# Args: colophon [extra_args...]
buc_tabtarget() {
  local z_colophon="$1"
  shift
  local z_extra="${*:+ $*}"
  zbuym_tt_path "${z_colophon}"
  test -n "${z_buym_tt_path}" || buc_die "buc_tabtarget: no tabtarget found for colophon '${z_colophon}'"
  buc_bare "        ${z_buym_tt_path}${z_extra}"
}

buc_context() {
  ZBUC_CONTEXT="$1"
}

# Enable trace to stderr safely if supported
zbuc_enable_trace() {
  # Only supported in Bash >= 4.1
  if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
    export BASH_XTRACEFD=2
  fi
  set -x
}

# Disable trace
zbuc_disable_trace() {
  set +x
}

zbuc_doc_mode_predicate() {
  test "${ZBUC_DOC_MODE}" = "true"
}

buc_doc_env() {
  set -e

  local env_var_name="${1}"
  local env_var_info="${2}"

  # Trim trailing spaces from variable name
  env_var_name="${env_var_name%% *}"

  # In doc mode, show documentation only (no validation — env vars may not be set)
  if zbuc_doc_mode_predicate; then
    zbuc_tint BUYC_MAGENTA "${1}"
    echo "  ${z_buym_format}:  ${env_var_info}"
    return 0
  fi

  # In execute mode, validate variable is set
  test -n "${!env_var_name:-}" || buc_warn "${env_var_name} is not set"
}

# Idiomatic last step of environment documentation in furnish.
# In doc mode, signals furnish to return early (sourcing/kindle not needed for help).
# Usage:
#    buc_doc_env_done || return 0
buc_doc_env_done() {
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  return 1
}

ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="UNFILLED"

buc_doc_brief() {
  set -e
  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  echo
  zbuc_tint BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"
  echo "  ${z_buym_format}"
  echo "    brief: $1"
}

buc_doc_lines() {
  set -e
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  echo "           $1"
}

buc_doc_param() {
  set -e
  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING} <<$1>>"
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  echo "    required: $1 - $2"
}

buc_doc_oparm() {
  set -e
  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING} [<<$1>>]"
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  echo "    optional: $1 - $2"
}

zbuc_usage() {
  zbuc_tint BUYC_CYAN "${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING}"
  printf '    usage: %s\n' "${z_buym_format}"
}

# Idiomatic last step of documentation in the bash api.
# Usage:
#    buc_doc_shown || return 0
buc_doc_shown() {
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  zbuc_usage
  return 1
}

buc_set_doc_mode() {
  ZBUC_DOC_MODE=true
}

buc_usage_die() {
  set -e
  local usage; usage=$(zbuc_usage)
  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
  zbuc_tint "" "${z_pfx} ${usage}"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}"
  exit 1
}

# Tint <text> through the buym core with the ambient named by <BUYC_name>
# (empty name => default/no ambient).  Sentinel-guarded and name-indirect so
# every BUYC_* dereference happens after kindle — a cold caller (doc-mode,
# pre-kindle buc_die) is set -u safe.  Resolved string lands in z_buym_format.
zbuc_tint() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  local z_name="${1:-}"
  local z_ambient=""
  test -z "${z_name}" || z_ambient="${!z_name}"
  buyf_format_yawp "${z_ambient}" "${2:-}"
}

# Multi-line print function with verbosity control.
# Usage: zbuc_print <min_verbosity> <BUYC_ambient_name> [message...]
# Sends output to stderr to avoid interfering with stdout returns.  Each
# message renders through the shared buym resolver (via zbuc_tint), so inline
# yawp spans resolve, the named ambient and gray operation sigil are
# terminal-aware, and a cold display does not trip set -u.
zbuc_print() {
  local min_verbosity="$1"
  local ambient_name="$2"
  shift 2

  # Always print if min_verbosity is -1, otherwise check BURE_VERBOSE
  if test "${min_verbosity}" -eq -1 || test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge "${min_verbosity}"; then
    zbuym_sentinel
    while test $# -gt 0; do
      zbuc_tint "${ambient_name}" "$1"
      if test -n "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"; then
        printf '%s%s%s %s\n' "${BUYC_GRAY}" "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}" "${BUYC_RESET}" "${z_buym_format}" >&2
      else
        printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2
      fi
      shift
    done
  fi
}

# Core logging implementation - always reads from stdin
zbuc_log() {
  test -n "${BURD_TRANSCRIPT:-}" || return 0

  local z_prefix="$1"
  local z_rest_prefix="$2"
  local z_outfile="${BURD_TRANSCRIPT}"

  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
    printf '%s%s\n' "${z_prefix}" "${z_line}" >> "${z_outfile}"
    z_prefix="${z_rest_prefix}"
  done
}


# Die if condition is true (non-zero)
# Usage: buc_die_if <condition> <message1> [<message2> ...]
buc_die_if() {
  local condition="$1"
  shift

  test "${condition}" -ne 0 || return 0

  set -e
  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
  zbuc_print -1 "" "${z_pfx} [$context] $1"
  shift
  zbuc_print -1 "" "$@"
  exit 1
}

# Die unless condition is true (zero)
# Usage: buc_die_unless <condition> <message1> [<message2> ...]
buc_die_unless() {
  local condition="$1"
  shift

  test "${condition}" -eq 0 || return 0

  set -e
  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
  zbuc_print -1 "" "${z_pfx} [$context] $1"
  shift
  zbuc_print -1 "" "$@"
  exit 1
}

zbuc_show_help() {
  local prefix="$1"
  local title="$2"
  local env_func="$3"

  echo "$title"
  echo

  if test -n "${env_func}"; then
    echo "Environment Variables:"
    "$env_func"
    echo
  fi

  echo "Commands:"

  local z_decl z_flag z_cmd
  while read -r z_decl z_flag z_cmd; do
    [[ "${z_cmd}" =~ ^${prefix}[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ ]] || continue
    buc_context "${z_cmd}"
    "${z_cmd}"
  done < <(declare -F)
}

buc_countdown() {
  local z_seconds="$1"
  local z_prompt="$2"

  if test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" = "skip"; then
    buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s (skipped — BURE_COUNTDOWN=skip)"
    return 0
  fi

  test -z "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"

  buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s to cancel (Ctrl-C)"
  sleep 1
  zbuc_tint BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW "${z_prompt}"
  printf '%s ' "${z_buym_format}" >/dev/tty
  local z_i
  for (( z_i=z_seconds; z_i>=1; z_i-- )); do
    printf '%d... ' "$z_i" >/dev/tty
    sleep 1
  done
  printf '\n' >/dev/tty
}

buc_require() {
  local z_prompt="$1"
  local z_required_value="$2"

  if test "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" = "skip"; then
    buc_step "Confirm: (skipped — BURE_CONFIRM=skip)"
    return 0
  fi

  test -z "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" || buc_die "BURE_CONFIRM must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_CONFIRM}'"

  sleep 1
  zbuc_tint BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW "${z_prompt}"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2
  printf 'Type %s to confirm: ' "${z_required_value}" >&2
  local z_input
  read -r z_input </dev/tty
  test "${z_input}" = "${z_required_value}" || buc_die "Confirmation failed — expected '${z_required_value}', got '${z_input}'"
}

buc_execute() {
  set -e
  local prefix="$1"
  local title="$2"
  local env_func="$3"
  local command="${4:-}"
  shift 3; test -z "${command}" || shift

  export BUC_VERBOSE="${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}"

  # Enable bash trace to stderr if BUC_VERBOSE is 3 or higher and bash >= 4.1
  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE}" -ge 3; then
    if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
      export PS4='+ ${BASH_SOURCE##*/}:${LINENO}: '
      export BASH_XTRACEFD=2
      set -x
    fi
  fi

  # Validate prefix pattern, furnish deps, then dispatch command
  if test -n "${command}" && [[ "${command}" =~ ^${prefix}[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ ]]; then
    buc_context "${command}"
    test -z "${env_func}" || "${env_func}" "${command}"
    declare -F "${command}" >/dev/null || buc_die "Function not found: ${command}"
    "${command}" "$@"
  else
    test -z "${command}" || buc_warn "Unknown command: ${command}"
    buc_set_doc_mode
    zbuc_show_help "${prefix}" "${title}" "${env_func}"
    echo
    exit 1
  fi
}

# --- Hyperlink helpers (OSC-8), falls back to plain text when disabled ---
# Disable with: export BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS=1
zbuc_hyperlink() {
  local z_text="${1:-}"
  local z_url="${2:-}"

  # ANSI codes for blue underlined text
  local z_blue_underline=$'\033[34m\033[4m'
  local z_reset=$'\033[0m'

  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
    # Fallback: blue underlined text with URL in angle brackets
    printf '%s%s%s <%s>' "${z_blue_underline}" "${z_text}" "${z_reset}" "${z_url}"
    return 0
  fi

  # OSC-8 with blue underline formatting
  printf '%s\033]8;;%s\033\\%s\033]8;;\033\\%s' \
    "${z_blue_underline}" "${z_url}" "${z_text}" "${z_reset}"
}

# buc_link "Prefix text" "Link text" "URL"  (prints to stderr like other user-visible messages)
buc_link() {
  local z_prefix="${1:-}"
  local z_text="${2:-}"
  local z_url="${3:-}"

  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_link    " "${z_prefix} ${z_text} -> ${z_url}"

  # Always show at verbosity >= 0 (same visibility as buc_step)
  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 0; then
    # Print prefix text if provided
    test -n "${z_prefix}" && printf '%s ' "${z_prefix}" >&2

    # Print formatted hyperlink
    zbuc_hyperlink "${z_text}" "${z_url}" >&2
    echo >&2
  fi
}

# --- Native path translation (Cygwin -> Windows-native) ---
#
# Normalize a path argument for a Windows-native tool (docker, cargo, ...)
# invoked from Cygwin bash. A Windows-native binary reads a Cygwin /cygdrive/X/...
# path as a literal Windows path and reports "does not exist"; hand it the
# drive-letter form (X:/... — forward slashes, which Windows accepts). Pure
# /cygdrive parameter expansion, no cygpath subshell, mirroring RBTDRX's Rust
# fast path. Gated on BURD_OSTYPE (the dispatch-synthesized platform fact): off
# Cygwin every path is emitted unchanged. A relative or already-native path
# passes through; a bare-absolute POSIX path (leading / but not /cygdrive) is an
# unsurveyed shape and returns 1 so the caller dies. Reads only its argument and
# BURD_OSTYPE — no kindle state — so it stays unit-testable in isolation.
#
# This is the single home for the transform: it folds the formerly-duplicated
# per-module copies (zrbfc/zrbndb/zrbob_native_path_capture) into one (heat ₣BV).
# Retire when the Windows-native tools build/run as true Cygwin binaries.
buc_native_path_capture() {
  local -r z_path="${1:?buc_native_path_capture: path required}"

  if test "${BURD_OSTYPE:-}" != "cygwin"; then
    printf '%s\n' "${z_path}"
    return 0
  fi

  case "${z_path}" in
    /cygdrive/?/*)
      local -r z_drive_rest="${z_path#/cygdrive/}"
      local -r z_drive="${z_drive_rest%%/*}"
      local -r z_drive_tail="${z_drive_rest#"${z_drive}/"}"
      printf '%s\n' "${z_drive}:/${z_drive_tail}"
      ;;
    /*)
      return 1
      ;;
    *)
      printf '%s\n' "${z_path}"
      ;;
  esac
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUTT Testbench - BUK test framework self-test
#
# Exercises the BUK test framework (bute/butr/butd/buto) with kick-tires
# and BURE tweak cases.  Pure local — no GCP, no containers, no network.

set -euo pipefail

BUTT_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
BUTT_BUTS_DIR="${BUTT_SCRIPT_DIR}/buts"

# Source dependencies
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bute_engine.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/butr_registry.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/butd_dispatch.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bure_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"

# Source test case files
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butckk_kick.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcbe_bure.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcbx_burx.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcfc_facts.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butclc_links.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcym_yelp.sh"

buc_context "${0##*/}"
zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle

######################################################################
# Registration

butt_kindle() {
  butr_kindle

  # All fixtures are pure local
  butr_suite_enroll "self-test"

  # kick-tires fixture (2 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "kick-tires" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "kick-tires" butckk_false_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "kick-tires" butckk_true_tcase

  # bure-tweak fixture (9 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "bure-tweak" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_empty_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_both_set_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_name_only_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_value_only_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_name_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_value_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_label_valid_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_label_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_unexpected_var_tcase

  # burx-exchange fixture (7 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "burx-exchange" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_dual_write_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_fields_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_preexist_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_timestamp_format_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_multi_dual_write_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_multi_preexist_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_multi_empty_content_tcase

  # fact-chaining fixture (5 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "fact-chaining" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "fact-chaining" butcfc_relay_forwards_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "fact-chaining" butcfc_relay_preserves_current_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "fact-chaining" butcfc_relay_idempotent_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "fact-chaining" butcfc_read_fact_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "fact-chaining" butcfc_read_fact_absent_tcase

  # buh-link fixture (3 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "buh-link" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_osc8_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_fallback_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_variants_tcase

  # buym-yelp fixture (15 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "buym-yelp" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_cmd_resolve_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_link_osc8_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_link_fallback_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_ambient_preservation_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_fast_path_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_multi_markers_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_plain_mode_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_gray_color_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_gray_plain_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_strip_cmd_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_strip_link_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_strip_href_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_strip_fast_path_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_cold_die_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_cold_die_plain_tcase
}

zbutt_noop_baste() {
  buto_trace "Baste (no-op)"
}

######################################################################
# Routing

butt_route() {
  local -r z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  zburd_sentinel

  butt_kindle

  export ZBUTE_ROOT_TEMP_DIR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"
  export BUT_VERBOSE="${BUT_VERBOSE:-0}"

  case "${z_command}" in
    buw-st)
      butd_run_suite "self-test"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown command: ${z_command}"
      ;;
  esac
}

butt_main() {
  local -r z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true
  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"
  butt_route "${z_command}" "$@"
}

butt_main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Test Engine - _tcase boundary runner

set -euo pipefail

# Source test-case API
ZBUTE_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
source "${ZBUTE_SCRIPT_DIR}/buto_operations.sh"

######################################################################
# _tcase boundary — case isolation subshell

# _tcase boundary runner: execute case function in isolation subshell
zbute_tcase() {
  set -e

  local z_case_name="${1}"
  declare -F "${z_case_name}" >/dev/null || buto_fatal "Test function not found: ${z_case_name}"

  buto_section "START: ${z_case_name}"

  local z_case_temp_dir="${ZBUTE_ROOT_TEMP_DIR}/${z_case_name}"
  mkdir -p "${z_case_temp_dir}" || buto_fatal "Failed to create test temp dir: ${z_case_temp_dir}"

  local z_status=0
  (
    set -e
    export BUT_TEMP_DIR="${z_case_temp_dir}"
    export BUTE_BURV_ROOT="${z_case_temp_dir}/burv"
    "${z_case_name}"
  ) || z_status=$?

  buto_trace "Ran: ${z_case_name} and got status:${z_status}"
  buto_fatal_on_error "${z_status}" "Test failed: ${z_case_name}"

  buto_trace "Finished: ${z_case_name} with status: ${z_status}"
  echo "${ZBUTO_GREEN}PASSED:${ZBUTO_RESET} ${z_case_name}" >&2
}

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUK Test Operations - Assertions, invocations, dispatch, and evidence
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	######################################################################
24	# Color codes
25	
26	buto_color() {
27	  if test -n "${TERM:-}" && test "${TERM}" != "dumb"; then
28	    printf '\033[%sm' "${1}"
29	  else
30	    printf ''
31	  fi
32	}
33	ZBUTO_WHITE=$(  buto_color '1;37' )
34	ZBUTO_RED=$(    buto_color '1;31' )
35	ZBUTO_GREEN=$(  buto_color '1;32' )
36	ZBUTO_RESET=$(  buto_color '0'    )
37	
38	######################################################################
39	# Generic renderer for aligned multi-line messages
40	# Usage: zbuto_render_lines PREFIX COLOR LINES...
41	
42	zbuto_render_lines() {
43	  local z_label="${1}"; shift
44	  local z_color="${1}"; shift
45	
46	  local z_prefix="${z_label}:"
47	
48	  local z_visible_prefix="${z_prefix}"
49	  test -z "${z_color}" || z_prefix="${z_color}${z_prefix}${ZBUTO_RESET}"
50	  local z_indent=""
51	  printf -v z_indent '%*s' "${#z_visible_prefix}" ''
52	
53	  local z_first=1
54	  local z_line=""
55	  for z_line in "$@"; do
56	    if test "${z_first}" -eq 1; then
57	      echo "${z_prefix} ${z_line}" >&2
58	      z_first=0
59	    else
60	      echo "${z_indent} ${z_line}" >&2
61	    fi
62	  done
63	}
64	
65	######################################################################
66	# Output functions
67	
68	buto_section() {
69	  test "${BUT_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 1 || return 0
70	  zbuto_render_lines "info " "${ZBUTO_WHITE}" "$@"
71	}
72	
73	buto_info() {
74	  zbuto_render_lines "info " "" "$@"
75	}
76	
77	buto_trace() {
78	  test "${BUT_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 2 || return 0
79	  zbuto_render_lines "trace" "" "$@"
80	}
81	
82	buto_fatal() {
83	  zbuto_render_lines "ERROR" "${ZBUTO_RED}" "$@"
84	  exit 1
85	}
86	
87	buto_fatal_on_error() {
88	  set -e
89	  local z_condition="${1}"; shift
90	  test "${z_condition}" -ne 0 || return 0
91	  buto_fatal "$@"
92	}
93	
94	buto_fatal_on_success() {
95	  set -e
96	  local z_condition="${1}"; shift
97	  test "${z_condition}" -eq 0 || return 0
98	  buto_fatal "$@"
99	}
100	
101	buto_success() {
102	  echo "${ZBUTO_GREEN}PASSED:${ZBUTO_RESET} $*" >&2
103	}
104	
105	######################################################################
106	# BURV bridge support
107	
108	# zbuto_next_invoke_capture() - Find next unused invoke number
109	# Scans BUTE_BURV_ROOT/invoke-NNNNN directories for next available slot
110	# Returns: next invoke number (starts at 10000)
111	zbuto_next_invoke_capture() {
112	  local z_burv_root="${BUTE_BURV_ROOT:-}"
113	  test -n "${z_burv_root}" || return 1
114	
115	  local z_invoke_num=10000
116	  while test -d "${z_burv_root}/invoke-${z_invoke_num}"; do
117	    z_invoke_num=$((z_invoke_num + 1))
118	  done
119	  echo "${z_invoke_num}"
120	}
121	
122	######################################################################
123	# Safely invoke a command under 'set -e', capturing stdout, stderr, and exit status
124	# Globals set:
125	#   ZBUTO_STDOUT       - command stdout
126	#   ZBUTO_STDERR       - command stderr
127	#   ZBUTO_STATUS       - command exit code
128	#   ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT      - BURV output root (empty if BURV not enabled)
129	#   ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR  - BURV output dir matching BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (empty if BURV not enabled)
130	# BURV bridge: If BUTE_BURV_ROOT is set, creates per-invocation BURV isolation
131	
132	zbuto_invoke() {
133	  buto_trace "Invoking: $*"
134	
135	  local z_tmp_stdout
136	  z_tmp_stdout=$(mktemp)
137	  local z_tmp_stderr
138	  z_tmp_stderr=$(mktemp)
139	
140	  # BURV bridge setup if enabled
141	  local z_burv_output=""
142	  local z_burv_temp=""
143	  if test -n "${BUTE_BURV_ROOT:-}"; then
144	    local z_invoke_num
145	    z_invoke_num=$(zbuto_next_invoke_capture)
146	    local z_invoke_dir="${BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-${z_invoke_num}"
147	    z_burv_output="${z_invoke_dir}/output"
148	    z_burv_temp="${z_invoke_dir}/temp"
149	    mkdir -p "${z_burv_output}" "${z_burv_temp}"
150	  fi
151	
152	  ZBUTO_STATUS=$( (
153	      set +e
154	      if test -n "${z_burv_output}"; then
155	        BURV_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR="${z_burv_output}" \
156	        BURV_TEMP_ROOT_DIR="${z_burv_temp}" \
157	          "$@" >"${z_tmp_stdout}" 2>"${z_tmp_stderr}"
158	      else
159	        "$@" >"${z_tmp_stdout}" 2>"${z_tmp_stderr}"
160	      fi
161	      printf '%s' "$?"
162	      exit 0
163	    ) || printf '__subshell_failed__' )
164	
165	  if test "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" = "__subshell_failed__" || test -z "${ZBUTO_STATUS}"; then
166	    ZBUTO_STATUS=127
167	    ZBUTO_STDOUT=""
168	    ZBUTO_STDERR="zbuto_invoke: command caused shell to exit before status could be captured"
169	  else
170	    ZBUTO_STDOUT=$(<"${z_tmp_stdout}")
171	    ZBUTO_STDERR=$(<"${z_tmp_stderr}")
172	  fi
173	
174	  rm -f "${z_tmp_stdout}" "${z_tmp_stderr}"
175	
176	  ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT="${z_burv_output}"
177	  ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR="${z_burv_output:+${z_burv_output}/current}"
178	}
179	
180	######################################################################
181	# buto_unit_* - Raw command invocation via zbuto_invoke
182	
183	buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout() {
184	  set -e
185	
186	  local z_expected="${1}"; shift
187	
188	  zbuto_invoke "$@"
189	
190	  buto_fatal_on_error "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Command failed with status ${ZBUTO_STATUS}" \
191	                                        "Command: $*"                               \
192	                                        "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
193	
194	  test "${ZBUTO_STDOUT}" = "${z_expected}" || buto_fatal "Output mismatch"            \
195	                                                         "Command: $*"                \
196	                                                         "Expected: '${z_expected}'"  \
197	                                                         "Got:      '${ZBUTO_STDOUT}'"
198	}
199	
200	buto_unit_expect_ok() {
201	  set -e
202	
203	  zbuto_invoke "$@"
204	
205	  buto_fatal_on_error "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Command failed with status ${ZBUTO_STATUS}" \
206	                                        "Command: $*"                               \
207	                                        "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
208	}
209	
210	buto_unit_expect_fatal() {
211	  set -e
212	
213	  zbuto_invoke "$@"
214	
215	  buto_fatal_on_success "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Expected failure but got success" \
216	                                          "Command: $*"                      \
217	                                          "STDOUT: ${ZBUTO_STDOUT}"          \
218	                                          "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
219	}
220	
221	######################################################################
222	# buto_tt_* - Tabtarget file invocation (requires tabtarget exists)
223	#
224	# Resolves colophon to tt/{colophon}.*.sh file, dies if missing.
225	# Extra args pass through to tabtarget script.
226	
227	zbuto_resolve_tabtarget() {
228	  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
229	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "zbuto_resolve_tabtarget: colophon required"
230	
231	  local z_tt_dir="${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR:-}"
232	  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buto_fatal "BURC_TABTARGET_DIR not set -- buto_tt requires BUK environment"
233	  local z_matches=("${z_tt_dir}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)
234	
235	  # Bash 3.2: no-match glob returns literal — check with test -e
236	  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buto_fatal "No tabtarget found for colophon '${z_colophon}' in ${z_tt_dir}/"
237	
238	  test "${#z_matches[@]}" -eq 1 || buto_fatal "Multiple tabtargets found for colophon '${z_colophon}' in ${z_tt_dir}/"
239	
240	  echo "${z_matches[0]}"
241	}
242	
243	buto_tt_expect_ok() {
244	  set -e
245	
246	  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
247	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "buto_tt_expect_ok: colophon required"
248	  shift
249	
250	  local z_tabtarget
251	  z_tabtarget=$(zbuto_resolve_tabtarget "${z_colophon}")
252	
253	  zbuto_invoke "${z_tabtarget}" "$@"
254	
255	  buto_fatal_on_error "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Tabtarget failed with status ${ZBUTO_STATUS}" \
256	                                        "Colophon: ${z_colophon}"                      \
257	                                        "Tabtarget: ${z_tabtarget}"                    \
258	                                        "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
259	}
260	
261	buto_tt_previous_output_capture() {
262	  test -n "${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR:-}" || buto_fatal "No previous tabtarget output"
263	  echo "${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR}"
264	}
265	
266	buto_tt_expect_fatal() {
267	  set -e
268	
269	  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
270	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "buto_tt_expect_fatal: colophon required"
271	  shift
272	
273	  local z_tabtarget
274	  z_tabtarget=$(zbuto_resolve_tabtarget "${z_colophon}")
275	
276	  zbuto_invoke "${z_tabtarget}" "$@"
277	
278	  buto_fatal_on_success "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Expected failure but got success"    \
279	                                          "Colophon: ${z_colophon}"             \
280	                                          "Tabtarget: ${z_tabtarget}"           \
281	                                          "STDOUT: ${ZBUTO_STDOUT}"             \
282	                                          "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
283	}
284	
285	######################################################################
286	# buto_launch_* - Workbench dispatch (no tabtarget file required)
287	#
288	# First arg is launcher (workbench), second is colophon, rest are args.
289	# Invokes launcher directly with colophon+args.
290	
291	buto_launch_expect_ok() {
292	  set -e
293	
294	  local z_launcher="${1:-}"
295	  local z_colophon="${2:-}"
296	  test -n "${z_launcher}" || buto_fatal "buto_launch_expect_ok: launcher required"
297	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "buto_launch_expect_ok: colophon required"
298	  shift 2
299	
300	  zbuto_invoke "${z_launcher}" "${z_colophon}" "$@"
301	
302	  buto_fatal_on_error "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Launch failed with status ${ZBUTO_STATUS}" \
303	                                        "Launcher: ${z_launcher}"                   \
304	                                        "Colophon: ${z_colophon}"                   \
305	                                        "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
306	}
307	
308	buto_launch_expect_fatal() {
309	  set -e
310	
311	  local z_launcher="${1:-}"
312	  local z_colophon="${2:-}"
313	  test -n "${z_launcher}" || buto_fatal "buto_launch_expect_fatal: launcher required"
314	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "buto_launch_expect_fatal: colophon required"
315	  shift 2
316	
317	  zbuto_invoke "${z_launcher}" "${z_colophon}" "$@"
318	
319	  buto_fatal_on_success "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Expected failure but got success" \
320	                                          "Launcher: ${z_launcher}"          \
321	                                          "Colophon: ${z_colophon}"          \
322	                                          "STDOUT: ${ZBUTO_STDOUT}"          \
323	                                          "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
324	}
325	
326	# eof
